title: cmd: support --config option
body:
Let's support --config option by setting environment variable DOCKER_CONFIG instead of ignoring it for docker compatibility.

Add test case for config option.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
pdoman run --config option is now supported.
```

comments.body:
Thanks @rhatdan for reviewing.

Changes in patch set 2:

1 Shore up git message.

2 Fix a comment typo in the test case.
what config are you exactly implying here?

> what config are you exactly implying here?

Hi, @baude, basically to support using podman's docker wrapper like this: sudo docker --config /etc/docker login

> I think we should keep doing what we're doing. Podman does not support Docker config, so I prefer the warning rather than giving the illusion of --config being properly supported.

@vrothberg but podman does support DOCKER_CONFIG env variable right now, even though it's not properly supported neither? Wouldn't this be a same case?

> @vrothberg but podman does support DOCKER_CONFIG env variable right now, even though it's not properly supported neither? Wouldn't this be a same case?

That's a fair point.  The DOCKER_CONFIG is used for authentication purposes.  If `--config` is documented as such I won't complain.

> > @vrothberg but podman does support DOCKER_CONFIG env variable right now, even though it's not properly supported neither? Wouldn't this be a same case?
> 
> That's a fair point. The DOCKER_CONFIG is used for authentication purposes. If `--config` is documented as such I won't complain.

@vrothberg good point, I will send a V3 documenting it.

Changes in patch set V3:

1 Rebased to the latest commit of main branch.

2 Modified commit message and help function, to state that this --config only for locating authentication file.
Changes LGTM, but linter is complaining.

Changes in patch set V4:

1 Rebased on the latest commit of main branch.

2 Fixed a invalid "fmt.Fprintf" usage that causing linter complaining.

/approve
/lgtm
/hold
Thanks @liuming50
@rhatdan I saw it still failed on some tests due to missing content in docs, so I made this V5, by adding "--config" description in docs/source/markdown/podman.1.md

Changes in patch set V7:

1 Add description in docs/source/markdown/podman.1.md, explain that only authentication parts are supported by "--config", as suggested by @vrothberg

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *liuming50*, *rhatdan*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/lgtm
Looks like --config needs a shell completion argument.

> Looks like --config needs a shell completion argument.

@rhatdan Thanks for the review, I have pushed a V8 to fix that, verified bash completion works on a qemuarm64 machine.

Changes in patch set V9:

1 Fixed a wrong order of global "--config" in test script.
Looks like your test is failing.

> Looks like your test is failing.

Yes, I am also aware of that, strange, I did not see why it's failing, the test passed if I run it manually with my docker hub account. I have resent the MR, see how it's going.

/lgtm
/unhold
